name: Caminhos do Montemuro (PR 1) — circular route
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alvarenga are set within the Arouca Geopark, a UNESCO-recognized region known for its dramatic geological formations and natural beauty. The landscape is characterized by steep slopes, sharp ridges, and deep ravines, with the Paiva River carving through gorges. White limestone rock and untamed white water rapids contribute to the area's distinctive terrain, offering a challenging environment for running. This region provides a diverse network of trails amidst its unique geological heritage. Best jogging routes around Alvarenga The most popular jogging route is Paiva Walkways, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This route offers an immersive experience through varied terrain, including waterfalls and rock formations along the Paiva River. Another top favourite among local runners is Caminhos do Montemuro (PR 1) — circular route, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This path features significant elevation changes and panoramic views of the dramatic geological formations. Local runners also love the Arouca – Arouca Town Center loop from Arouca, a 19.0 miles (30.5 km) trail leading through the broader Arouca Geopark's natural areas, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Jogging around Alvarenga is defined by steep slopes, sharp ridges, and river gorges, with routes often traversing the unique geological features of the Arouca Geopark. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging trails with significant elevation gain. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Alvarenga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Alvarenga?
A: Running routes in Alvarenga are primarily set within the UNESCO-recognized Arouca Geopark, characterized by dramatic geological formations. You can expect steep slopes, sharp ridges, and deep ravines, with the Paiva River carving through gorges. The landscape features white limestone rock and untamed white water rapids, offering a challenging and visually stunning environment for runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Alvarenga?
A: Yes, Alvarenga offers circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2185829032/caminhos-do-montemuro-pr-1-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminhos do Montemuro (PR 1) — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 11.5-mile (18.5 km) path featuring significant elevation changes and panoramic views of the dramatic geological formations.

Q: What are some of the natural attractions I can see while jogging in Alvarenga?
A: While jogging in Alvarenga, you can experience several natural attractions. The renowned &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/723208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paiva Walkways&lt;/a&gt; offer an immersive experience along the Paiva River, featuring waterfalls and rock formations. You might also encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7227290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gola do Salto (Rio Paiva)&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful river gorge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Alvarenga?
A: While many routes in Alvarenga are challenging, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2181630163/paiva-walkways" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paiva Walkways&lt;/a&gt; offer a more accessible and immersive experience through varied terrain, including beaches, waterfalls, and rock formations. Its established trail provides a scenic route that can be enjoyed by families, though some sections may still require a good fitness level.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Alvarenga?
A: For routes like the Paiva Walkways, there are designated entrances that typically offer parking. Specific entry points include Passadiços do Paiva Trailhead (Areinho) and Passadiços do Paiva - Entrada Espiunca. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most convenient parking locations.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes in Alvarenga?
A: The running routes in Alvarenga are generally considered challenging. Out of 13 available routes, 11 are rated as 'difficult' on komoot. This is due to the region's steep slopes, significant elevation changes, and rugged terrain within the Arouca Geopark.

Q: Are there any bridges or unique structures to see on the running trails?
A: Yes, the region is home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5957874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;516 Arouca Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, one of the world's longest pedestrian suspension bridges. This bridge connects to the Paiva Walkways, offering a thrilling experience and expanding possibilities for routes with breathtaking views. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3755382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge over the Paiva River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many running routes are there in Alvarenga?
A: There are 13 running routes available around Alvarenga on komoot, offering a diverse network of trails for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, though most are rated as difficult.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alvarenga?
A: Runners often praise the unique geological features and the dramatic natural beauty of the Arouca Geopark. The immersive experience along the Paiva River, with its waterfalls and rock formations, is frequently highlighted as a favorite aspect of jogging in Alvarenga.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Alvarenga traverse the region's sharp ridges and steep slopes, providing panoramic views of the dramatic geological formations and the Paiva River gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2185829032/caminhos-do-montemuro-pr-1-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminhos do Montemuro (PR 1) — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its extensive vistas.

Q: Is there a route that passes through Arouca Town Center?
A: Yes, there is a route that includes the town center. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22504184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arouca – Arouca Town Center loop from Arouca&lt;/a&gt; is a 19.0-mile (30.5 km) trail that leads through the broader Arouca Geopark's natural areas and passes through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arouca Town Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature a suspension bridge?
A: Yes, the area is famous for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/623937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paiva Walkways and 516 Arouca Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. While jogging the Paiva Walkways, you'll have the opportunity to experience the thrilling 516 Arouca Bridge, which offers spectacular views over the gorge.
tips:
On the vast plateaus of Montemuro, time can be seen in stone. Between moss-covered mounds, solitary menhirs and ancient sacred circles, the landscape tells stories that date back to prehistoric times.

Near the wind farm, this ancient presence lives on. The Marco da Jogada and the tombs next to the chapel of São Pedro do Campo are just some of the landmarks that unite past and present.

High up in Alvarenga, the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Mount offers a peaceful retreat, with a picnic area and stunning views of the region.

Every September 8, devotion takes over the place for the festival in honor of Our Lady of the Mount. The sanctuary is easily accessible, and can even be reached by car.

waypoints.name: Our Lady of the Mount Chapel
